content: Frozen storage significantly reduces folate levels in vegetables, with peas losing 20% after 3 months and 96% after 6 months.
Spinach retains 20% of its folate after 6 months of freezing but loses 80% after 12 months, making it nearly useless as a folate source.
Broccoli loses 93% of its folate within 3 months of freezing, while cauliflower loses 98% within the same period.
Green beans are more stable, retaining 80% of their folate after 6 months and 20% after 9-12 months of freezing.
Liver maintains 80% of its folate after 6 months of freezing, making it a reliable source of folate despite storage.
Cooking methods often reduce folate levels, but liver sausage retains its folate content during cooking due to the matrix and enzymes in the tissue.
